content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.procedural_policy.v1",
  "name": "cli_arg_length_policy",
  "goal": "Reduce Windows CLI argument-length risk by preferring file-based inputs and short commands.",
  "rules": [
    "If a command or tool call would exceed ~7000 characters total, write payloads to temp files and pass file paths instead of inline content.",
    "Prefer JSON/file inputs over long inline strings for LLM prompts, configs, or data blobs.",
    "When assembling commands, avoid repeating long flags; use config files or environment variables if available.",
    "Keep one command per exec; avoid chaining with long heredocs on Windows."
  ],
  "updated_at": 1773201449
}
